summary: Tested MotoProf PostgreSQL migration. 22/22 backend tests pass. Fixed critical frontend bug: AuthContext.login/register was setting user to full response {message, user} instead of data.user, causing Navbar crash (Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')) after login. All major flows verified.

minor.issue: Returns {product_ids: []} object instead of array — frontend WishlistContext should handle this format

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED: login() and register() were calling setUser(data) but backend returns {message, user: {...}}. Fixed to setUser(data.user || data). This caused Navbar crash on login.

issues: Cart total shows 0,00₺ after direct navigation (cart state lost on page reload - expected behavior for localStorage cart)

action_items: Verify WishlistContext.js handles {product_ids: []} response format from GET /api/wishlist (not an array)
Consider adding SameSite=None for cookie if CORS issues arise in production (currently samesite=lax)
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.js: login/register setUser(data) bug FIXED - was using full response wrapper instead of data.user
GET /api/wishlist returns {product_ids:[]} not a plain array - WishlistContext must account for this
server.py: bcrypt warning 'module bcrypt has no attribute __about__' - harmless but indicates library version mismatch
Auth cookie uses samesite=lax which is correct for same-origin but verify in production HTTPS context
seed_data_creation: PostgreSQL DB seeded with admin user, 25 demo products, and 4 coupons (MOTO10, ILKALIS, KARGO50, WELCOME)
context_for_next_testing_agent: PostgreSQL migration complete. Auth is cookie-based (httponly). Critical bug fixed: AuthContext login/register now properly extracts data.user from response. All 22 backend tests pass. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_motoprof_postgresql.py
rca_of_issue: Root cause: Backend login/register endpoints return {message: string, user: {...}} but AuthContext.js was calling setUser(data) where data is the full axios response body. When Navbar accessed user.name[0] it got undefined.name which crashed. Fix: use setUser(data.user || data) to extract nested user object.
